posts.text: TRUMP SAYS DEAL WITH IRAN IS NOW COMPLETE
posts.author: zerohedge

posts.text: Booming exports are propping up China’s growth, allowing policymakers to delay tougher measures to restore consumer confidence. Its leaders should do more to diversify their country’s sources of demand  https://www.economist.com/leaders/2026/06/11/for-its-own-sake-china-should-change-its-growth-model?taid=6a2f1fb4c0a91f000130466b&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
BREAKING: President Trump says the deal with Iran is “now complete.”

“I hereby fully authorize the toll free opening of the Strait of Hormuz… let the oil flow,” Trump says.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text: Pretty much. Foundational models are overpowered. The 2025 vintage datacenters will be obsolete next year (remember how cute those 2016 fracs at 3500ft and just a touch of Wisconsin white sand were??). Now compare to 4 mile laterals, multi-fracked with as much brown sand as you can jam in the thing. A decade later, and still no one really makes money in shale. They just are monetizing low cost basis land, and doing it poorly…
posts.author: hkuppy
